task: hexa-native C backend compile spike for CLM phi_cache_loader hot loop
subcommand: hexa build <file.hexa> -o <out> — emits C via self/native/hexa_v2 then clang -O2

smoke_result.codegen_gap_found: implicit-tail-return fn bodies emit `expr; return hexa_void();` instead of `return expr;`. Workaround = explicit `return` keyword.

phi_cache_loader_hexa.self_test_native: T2-T6 PASS (zero_vec, lookup stub, anchor_project, contrastive_loss, state_hash). T1 phi_cache_new + T7 phi_cache_load FAIL with `map key 'loaded' not found` — struct literal field init codegen bug in no-arg fns

bench_phi_cache_speedup_hexa.run_result: SUCCESS — full 1000-iter hot loop executes, hit_count=1000/1000, struct field access `cache.n_entries` WORKS in hot path (phi_cache_load populates struct correctly, only phi_cache_new() empty-init path hits the codegen bug)

speedup_note: 393.8x native vs same-machine interpreter, measured at seq=64 d_model=128 iters=1000 on the exact flag-ON bucket-hit path (phi_state_bucket_id + phi_cache_lookup_by_state_id + phi_anchor_project + phi_contrastive_loss). Exceeds 10x target by ~40x.

accumulated_loss.drift_source: clang -O2 fp reassoc vs interp eval order — correctness OK

known_codegen_gaps_not_blocking_spike: implicit tail-return: fn body with bare expression as last stmt compiles to `expr; return void;` — must use explicit `return` keyword
struct literal field init in no-arg fn body (e.g. phi_cache_new) produces map-backed struct that loses field names at first access; struct pop from loader path (via file parse) works fine
native self-test at startup emits 5 spurious `map key 'X' not found` warnings before bench begins — from phi_cache_new() calls inside self-test, does not affect bench timing
next_unblock: fix hexa→C codegen for (a) implicit tail-return fn bodies and (b) struct literal field init in no-arg fns. Both sit in self-host compiler self/native/hexa_v2 emit_fn_body / emit_struct_literal. Until fixed, prefer explicit `return` + struct values flowing through file-parse / arg-passing paths — which is exactly the hot loop shape phi_cache_loader already has, so CLM can adopt native build TODAY.
adoption_path_for_clm: 1) Build phi_cache_loader bundle via `hexa build training/train_clm.hexa -o build/train_clm_native` 2) Verify struct fields are loaded via phi_cache_load (not phi_cache_new) on the critical path 3) Swap hexa run → native binary in training CLI. Expected per-step cache-block latency: 111µs instead of 43.8ms — frees 43.7ms × steps for real work.

rules_honored.R1_HEXA_FIRST: only hexa tooling invoked, no hand-written .c
rules_honored.stage0_unmodified: hexa binary used as-is, no rebuild attempted
rules_honored.background_tasks: bench short enough to run foreground
